information: Protein is essential for muscle building and repair. Aim for 0.7-1g protein per pound of body weight for muscle growth.
Rest days are crucial for muscle recovery and growth. Muscles grow during rest periods, not during workouts.
Compound exercises like squats, deadlifts, and bench press work multiple muscle groups and are most efficient for building strength.
Moderate cardio 3-4 times per week combined with strength training is effective for fat loss. Too much cardio can hinder muscle growth.
Eat a balanced meal with carbs and protein 1-2 hours before training for optimal energy and performance.
Consume protein within 30-60 minutes after a workout to support muscle recovery and growth.
Drink at least 8 glasses of water daily. During intense workouts, you need more to replace fluids lost through sweat.
To build muscle, consistently increase weight, reps, or sets over time. This principle is key to continued progress.
Train each muscle group 2-3 times per week for optimal growth. Allow 48 hours between training the same muscle group.
Aim for 7-9 hours of sleep nightly. Most muscle recovery and growth hormone release happens during deep sleep.
